title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 8: Geometry and Numbers
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Leo has 24 marbles. He gives 7 to his brother and 5 to his sister. How many marbles does Leo have left?
questions.solution: Leo gives away 7 + 5 = 12 marbles. He has 24 - 12 = 12 marbles left.

questions.question_text: How many small squares are shaded in the grid below?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='70' width='30' height='30' fill='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 3 by 3 grid with a checkerboard pattern where 5 squares are shaded.
questions.solution: Counting the shaded squares in the checkerboard pattern: 2 + 1 + 2 = 5.

questions.question_text: An apple costs 2 dollars and a pear costs 3 dollars. Sara buys 4 apples and 2 pears. How much does she pay?
questions.solution: Apples cost 4 x 2 = 8 dollars. Pears cost 2 x 3 = 6 dollars. Total is 8 + 6 = 14 dollars.

questions.question_text: Find the missing number in the sequence: 40, 35, 30, __, 20, 15.
questions.solution: Each number decreases by 5. So 30 - 5 = 25.

questions.question_text: Tom starts reading at 2:40 pm and stops at 3:25 pm. How many minutes did he read?
questions.image_description: Two clock faces, the first showing 2:40 and the second showing 3:25.
questions.solution: From 2:40 pm to 3:00 pm is 20 minutes. From 3:00 pm to 3:25 pm is 25 minutes. Total: 20 + 25 = 45 minutes.

questions.question_text: How many sides does the shape shown below have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,15 100,40 90,90 30,90 20,40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A pentagon with five straight sides.
questions.solution: The pentagon shown has 5 straight sides.

questions.question_text: Ana is taller than Beth. Beth is taller than Clara. Who is the shortest of the three?
questions.solution: Ana > Beth > Clara in height, so Clara is the shortest.

questions.question_text: What number do you get when you add the smallest two-digit number to the largest one-digit number?
questions.solution: Smallest two-digit number is 10 and largest one-digit number is 9. Sum: 10 + 9 = 19.

questions.question_text: The rectangle shown is divided into equal squares. How many squares are inside the rectangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='140' height='60'/><line x1='45' y1='10' x2='45' y2='70'/><line x1='80' y1='10' x2='80' y2='70'/><line x1='115' y1='10' x2='115' y2='70'/><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='150' y2='40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle split by three vertical lines and one horizontal line into equal smaller squares.
questions.solution: There are 4 columns and 2 rows of equal squares, giving 4 x 2 = 8 small squares.

questions.question_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Jake eats 3 slices and Lily eats 2 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='90'/><line x1='10' y1='50' x2='90' y2='50'/><line x1='22' y1='22' x2='78' y2='78'/><line x1='78' y1='22' x2='22' y2='78'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A circle divided into 8 equal slices like a pizza.
questions.solution: They ate 3 + 2 = 5 slices out of 8. Remaining: 8 - 5 = 3 slices, which is 3/8 of the pizza.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive whole numbers is 30. What is the largest of these three numbers?
questions.solution: The middle number is 30 / 3 = 10. The three numbers are 9, 10, 11. The largest is 11.

questions.question_text: A ribbon is 120 cm long. It is cut into pieces that are each 15 cm long. How many pieces are made?
questions.solution: Dividing: 120 / 15 = 8 pieces.

questions.question_text: Look at the triangles shown. If the pattern continues, how many small triangles will be in the 5th figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='20,50 30,30 40,50'/><polygon points='70,50 80,30 90,50'/><polygon points='90,50 100,30 110,50'/><polygon points='130,50 140,30 150,50'/><polygon points='150,50 160,30 170,50'/><polygon points='170,50 180,30 190,50'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Figure 1 has 1 triangle, figure 2 has 2 triangles, figure 3 has 3 triangles.
questions.solution: The pattern adds one triangle each time: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. The 5th figure has 5 triangles.

questions.question_text: In a box there are red, blue, and green balls. There are twice as many red balls as blue balls, and 3 green balls. If there are 5 blue balls, how many balls are in the box in total?
questions.solution: Red: 2 x 5 = 10. Blue: 5. Green: 3. Total: 10 + 5 + 3 = 18.

questions.question_text: Which figure below has exactly 4 right angles?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='10,20 50,20 50,60 10,60'/><polygon points='70,60 90,20 110,60'/><polygon points='130,40 150,20 170,40 150,60'/><circle cx='210' cy='40' r='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four shapes: a rectangle, a triangle, a rhombus, and a circle.
questions.solution: A rectangle has 4 corners, each a right angle (90 degrees). The other shapes do not.

questions.question_text: Noah has 40 dollars. He spends one quarter of it on a book. How much money does Noah have left?
questions.solution: One quarter of 40 is 40 / 4 = 10 dollars. Noah has 40 - 10 = 30 dollars left.

questions.question_text: The figure is made of 5 identical squares joined edge to edge in a row. If each square has a side of 2 cm, what is the total perimeter of the whole figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='90' y='10' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Five unit squares arranged in a horizontal row, each sharing a side with the next.
questions.solution: The long row is 10 cm (5 x 2) and 2 cm tall. Perimeter: 2 x (10 + 2) = 24 cm.

questions.question_text: Four children sit in a row. Jack is not at either end. Kate sits directly to the right of Jack. Liam sits on the far left, and Mia is the fourth child. Who sits on the far right?
questions.solution: Liam is far left. Jack is not at an end, so Jack sits in position 2 or 3. Kate sits to Jack's right. Testing position 3 for Jack: Liam, Mia, Jack, Kate works; so Kate sits on the far right.

questions.question_text: A number plus 18 equals three times the same number. What is the number?
questions.solution: Let the number be n. Then n + 18 = 3n, so 18 = 2n, giving n = 9.

questions.question_text: A clock is shown at 3 o'clock sharp. What is the smaller angle between the hour hand and the minute hand?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='60' cy='60' r='50'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='60' y2='20'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='95' y2='60'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A clock face showing 3 o'clock with the hour hand pointing right and the minute hand pointing up.
questions.solution: At 3 o'clock, the minute hand is on 12 and the hour hand is on 3. That is one quarter of a full turn, which is 90 degrees.

questions.question_text: The numbers 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 are written in a circle in order. Starting just after 1 and moving clockwise, every second remaining number is crossed out until only one remains. Which number is the last one left?
questions.solution: Cross out 2, keep 3, cross 4, keep 5, cross 1, keep 3, cross 5. The last remaining number is 3.

questions.question_text: A large jug holds 6 liters of juice. Small cups hold 250 mL each. How many small cups can be filled completely from one full jug?
questions.solution: 6 liters equals 6000 mL. Divide by 250: 6000 / 250 = 24 cups.

questions.question_text: A square is divided by both of its diagonals. How many triangles of any size can be found in the figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='80' height='80'/><line x1='10' y1='10' x2='90' y2='90'/><line x1='90' y1='10' x2='10' y2='90'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A square with both diagonals drawn, crossing at the center.
questions.solution: The diagonals form 4 small triangles. Combining adjacent pairs gives 4 larger triangles. Total: 4 + 4 = 8 triangles.

questions.question_text: Three friends each have a different favorite color among red, blue, and yellow. Ivy does not like red. Owen does not like blue or yellow. Zoe likes yellow. What is Ivy's favorite color?
questions.solution: Owen likes only red (not blue or yellow). Zoe likes yellow. So Ivy's only remaining choice is blue.
